titleStmt.title: Letter from Andrew M. Harper to Matthew Nathan, 1 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Andrew M. Harper

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from a retired resident magistrate, Andrew M Harper (1851-), to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) the under-secretary for Ireland. Nathan was under-secretary until the 1916 Rising after which he offered his resignation. In the letter Harper is informing Nathan that his address will change from the next fourteen days.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/18262/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Merton, Kilkee. 1 Novr., 1915. Sir With reference
to papers 15532
20: September 1915,
and previous
correspondence
on same subject,
I beg to inform
you that I am
leaving above address
for good. My address for
the next fourteen
days will be :
Maples Hotel,
Kildare Street,
Dublin. I am, Sir,
Your obt. Servant A.M.Harper
Retired R.M. The Under Secretary
etc. etc.
